metadata.purpose: Prediction of potential user questions and attribute definitions for knowledge graph
metadata.note: These attributes are NOT part of the existing knowledge graph but represent expected query patterns

layer0_attributes.description: Unique identifier assigned to each project in the knowledge graph. Essential for referencing specific projects across all queries. Used as primary key for data integrity and audit trails.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: List all Project IDs from the dataset.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show all project codes; List unique project identifiers; What projects exist? | Can you give me the ID numbers of projects in your database?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: IDs are unique and non-null. Format is standardized (numeric). No duplicate IDs exist in the system.

layer0_attributes.description: Official registered name of the real estate project as per RERA documentation. This is the marketing name used by developers and essential for project identification in reports and communications.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: List the names of the projects.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: List all project names; Show me the projects you have; What are the project names? | Give me the list of all projects in your database with their names.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Project names are official and standardized. Names match RERA registration documents. No special characters issues.

layer0_attributes.description: Legal name of the developer/builder who is executing the project. Includes registered company name and is critical for partner analysis, reputation tracking, and relationship management in the real estate portfolio.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Who are the builders? Show developer names; List all developers; Which companies are building these projects? | Can you tell me which developers are in your portfolio?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Developer names are standardized and match company registration. One developer per project primary assignment. Historical developer changes are noted separately.

layer0_attributes.description: Geographical micro-market where the project is located. Standardized location name used for market segmentation, competitor analysis, and location-based price benchmarking. Crucial for market comparisons.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Which locations are covered? Show all micro-markets; Where are projects located? | List locations of all projects. What areas do you cover?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Location names are standardized (standardized micro-market names). Each project maps to exactly one location. Location boundaries are consistent across queries.

layer0_attributes.description: Official project launch date when the project became available for sales. This temporal marker is essential for market entry analysis, project lifecycle tracking, and competitive positioning assessment.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: When did projects launch? Show launch dates; When were projects started for sale? | Can you tell me when each project started selling units?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Date format is MM-YYYY (consistent). Launch date = first sales opening date. Before this date, no sales were recorded.

layer0_attributes.description: Expected or actual project handover date as per RERA registration. Represents when buyers will receive possession of units. Critical for timeline planning, investor returns calculation, and project status tracking.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: When will projects be delivered? Show possession dates; Expected delivery timeline? | Tell me when units will be ready for handover.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Date format is MM-YYYY. Based on RERA registrations (official dates). Represents expected delivery, not actual if project is ongoing.

layer0_attributes.description: Total number of housing units in the entire project (all phases combined for multi-phase developments). This represents total market supply and is fundamental for market penetration and absorption rate calculations.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt:
What is the project size of X project ?  
What is the Avg project size in Y location ?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many units in total? Total project size? How many apartments/homes? | What is the total supply of units in each project?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Size includes all marketable units. Multi-phase projects: all units included. Parking, commercial spaces excluded from count.

layer0_attributes.description: Total number of units that have been launched and made available for sale (marketable supply). May differ from Project Size if phased launch is occurring. Used for market penetration and inventory calculations.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is total supply? How many units launched? Units in market? | Tell me how many units are available for sale in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Supply = Launched units. Includes open units only (not yet sold). Does not include pre-launch or future phases.

layer0_attributes.description: Percentage of total launched supply that has been sold (units booked/completed). Key metric for market absorption and investor confidence. Calculated as (Sold Units / Total Supply) × 100.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What % is sold? Show sell-through rates; How much has sold? | What percentage of units have been sold in each project?
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Sold% = Booked + Completed units only. Does not include pre-booking. Represents market response and project attractiveness.

layer0_attributes.description: Percentage of total supply remaining unsold (available for purchase). Represents market opportunity and risk factor. Calculated as (Unsold Units / Total Supply) × 100. Complements Sold%.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What % remains? Show remaining inventory; How much is unsold? | Tell me what percentage of units are still available in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Unsold% = 100% - Sold%. Represents current market inventory. Does not include withdrawn units or held inventory.

layer0_attributes.description: Units sold in last 12 months.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many units sold annually? Last 12 months sales? Yearly sales units? | Tell me the total units sold in the last 12 months.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: 12-month period.

layer0_attributes.description: Annual revenue.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is annual revenue? Last year revenue? Yearly sales value? | Tell me the total revenue collected in the last 12 months.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Value in Crores.

layer0_attributes.description: Average saleable area per unit measured in square feet. Includes carpet area plus proportionate common area. Fundamental for price normalization (PSF calculations) and buyer preference analysis across unit types.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is average unit size? How big are the units? Average area per apartment? | Tell me the average saleable area of units in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Size = Saleable area (includes common area proportionate share). Represents average across all unit types (1BHK, 2BHK, etc.). Weighted by unit count if varied.

layer0_attributes.description: Launch price per sqft.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Initial PSF
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Launch-time price.

layer0_attributes.description: Current saleable PSF.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Market PSF
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Latest price.

layer0_attributes.description: Percentage of total supply absorbed per month (monthly absorption rate). Indicates market pace and demand intensity. Critical for timeline projections and market heat assessment.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is monthly absorption rate? How fast are units selling? Monthly sales %? | Tell me what percentage of units sell each month on average.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% applied uniformly on Total Supply. Based on last 12-month trend. Assumes no significant seasonal variation going forward.

layer0_attributes.description: Regulatory compliance indicator showing if the project is registered under Real Estate Regulation Act (RERA). Mandatory for all residential projects in India. Critical for legal and investment risk assessment.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Is it RERA registered? Compliance status? Legal approval status? | Tell me which projects are registered with RERA for legal compliance.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Y = Registered with RERA authority. N = Not registered (high risk). Data reflects current status. Registration status is verified.

layer0_attributes.description: Absolute count of units remaining unsold (still available for purchase). Calculated from Total Supply and Unsold%. Represents current market inventory. Critical for absorption and time-to-sell calculations.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many units left? Remaining inventory count? Available units? | Tell me how many units are still unsold in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Unsold = Total Supply × Unsold%. Formula accuracy depends on Unsold% precision. Represents current state at snapshot date.

layer0_attributes.description: Total count of units sold to date (booked or completed). Derived from Total Supply and Sold%. Represents market absorption progress. Used in revenue calculations and performance tracking.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many sold? Total sales count? How many have been purchased? | Tell me the total number of units sold in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Sold = Total Supply × Sold%. Includes only confirmed bookings/completed sales. Excludes cancellations (net of returns).

layer0_attributes.description: Monthly absorption from annual sales.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Monthly absorption
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Annual Sales = last 12 months.

layer0_attributes.description: Units sold per month from velocity %.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Actual monthly sales
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% applies on total supply.

layer0_attributes.description: Time to exhaust unsold stock.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Inventory months
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Absorption steady.

layer0_attributes.description: % growth since launch.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: % appreciation
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Launch PSF accurate.

layer0_attributes.description: Actual achieved PSF.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Achieved PSF
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Revenue accurate.

layer0_attributes.description: Average revenue per sold unit.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: avg ticket
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Annual revenue accurate.

layer0_attributes.description: Value of remaining stock.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: stock valuation
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: PSF×Area holds constant.

layer0_attributes.description: Projected time required to sell remaining unsold units at current absorption rate. Indicates market demand strength and project runway. Calculated as Unsold Units / Annual Sales rate.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: How long to sell remaining stock?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How long to sell remaining? Months to clear inventory? Project runway? | Tell me how long it will take to sell all remaining units.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month sales pace (assumed constant). Excludes seasonality impact. Assumes no marketing intensity changes.

layer0_attributes.description: Average price per unit at current rates.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: unit pricing
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Uses current PSF.

layer0_attributes.description: Price per unit at launch.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: launch pricing
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Uses launch PSF.

layer0_attributes.description: Difference between current and launch PSF.
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: pricing delta
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Both PSF values accurate.

layer0_attributes.description: Current lifecycle stage of the project (Pre-Launch, Ongoing Sales, Nearing Completion, Ready to Occupy, Completed/Possession Ongoing). Indicates project maturity and investment risk level. Essential for portfolio segmentation and market positioning.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: What is the current status of the projects?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show project statuses; Which projects are under construction? Which are selling now? Which are completed? | Tell me the current stage of each project (selling, completed, etc.)
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Status reflects current project phase. Updated quarterly or after major milestone. Status classifications are standardized.
layer0_attributes.sample_values: Ongoing Sales, Ready to Occupy, Completed
layer0_attributes.expected_answer: Ongoing Sales, Ready to Occupy, Completed

layer0_attributes.description: Total development/construction cost of the entire project estimated or actual. Fundamental for developer profitability analysis, ROI calculations, and capital requirements assessment. Includes land + construction.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: What is the total project cost?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show total development cost; How much was spent building? Project investment amount? | Tell me the total cost to develop each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Cost in Rs Crores. Includes land acquisition + construction + approvals. Based on developer estimates or audited data.

layer0_attributes.description: Total saleable area of all units combined (all phases). Represents total marketable area in square feet. Used for area-based metrics, price normalization, and capacity analysis.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: What is the total saleable area in the project?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show total project area; Total saleable sq.ft.? How big is the entire project? | Tell me the total area available for sale across all units.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Area = Sum of all unit saleable areas. Measured in square feet. Includes proportionate common area per unit.

layer0_attributes.description: Total revenue realized from unit sales to date. Includes all booked and completed sales. Critical for cash flow analysis, payment milestone tracking, and project financial health assessment.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: How much revenue has been collected?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show total sales revenue; How much money collected? Total cash realized? | Tell me the total revenue from selling units in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Revenue = Sold Units × Average Realized Price. Based on RERA filings or company records. Includes GST.

layer0_attributes.description: Classification of project by housing type (Residential, Mixed-Use, Commercial). Indicates primary market segment and buyer profile. Important for market comparisons and strategy positioning.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: What is the project category or type?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show project types; Is it residential or commercial? What kind of project? | Tell me the category of each project (residential, mixed-use, etc.).
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Category = Primary property type. Standard classifications used. One primary category per project.

layer0_attributes.description: Distribution of unit types in the project (1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, etc. percentages or counts). Indicates target buyer segment and market positioning. Important for buyer demographic analysis.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: What is the BHK mix in the project?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show unit mix; How many 1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK? What types of units? | Tell me the breakdown of unit types (bedroom count) in each project.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: BHK = Bedroom-Hall-Kitchen configuration. Mix represents as % or count. Reflects market positioning (affordable vs premium).

layer0_attributes.description: Count of units that have received or are near possession. Indicates project maturity and immediate investment opportunity. Important for inventory analysis and buyer decision-making.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: How many units are ready to occupy?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show units ready for possession; How many are delivered? RTO units count? | Tell me how many units are ready or soon ready to move in.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: RTO = In possession or within 3 months of possession. Represents deliverable inventory. Does not include defects list items.

layer0_attributes.description: Count of units still under construction (not yet in defects list stage). Indicates construction progress and remaining project completion timeline. Used for project status assessment.
layer0_attributes.sample_prompt: How many units are under construction?
layer0_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show under construction count; How many units being built? In-progress inventory? | Tell me how many units are still being constructed.
layer0_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Under Construction = Structural work ongoing. Does not include completed or possession-ready units. Based on site reports.
layer0_attributes.formula_derivation: Project Size - RTO Units - Completed Units

layer1_attributes.description: Remaining unsold units.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Remaining units
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Unsold% and Supply accurate.

layer1_attributes.description: Total sold units.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Units sold till date
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Sold% accurate.

layer1_attributes.description: Average monthly unit absorption derived from annual sales data. Indicates consistent monthly demand and market pace. Critical for forecasting and pipeline planning.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many units sell per month? Monthly absorption? Average monthly sales? | Tell me the average number of units sold each month.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month actual sales divided by 12. Assumes uniform distribution across months. Does not account for seasonality.

layer1_attributes.description: Time duration required to clear remaining unsold inventory at current absorption pace. Key indicator of market strength and project runway. MOI < 6 months indicates strong demand.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How long to sell remaining? Months of inventory remaining? Project runway? | Tell me how many months it will take to sell all unsold units.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month pace (assumed constant going forward). Excludes seasonality and marketing spikes. Absorption steady assumption.

layer1_attributes.description: Percentage appreciation in unit price from launch to current date. Indicates market value growth and investment returns. Positive growth shows market strength and buyer confidence.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is price appreciation? % price growth? How much have prices increased? | Tell me the percentage price increase from launch till now.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Launch PSF vs Current PSF comparison. Assumes consistent unit sizes and locations. Market-driven appreciation only.

layer1_attributes.description: Actual average price per square foot achieved through sales to date. Reflects real market realization vs launch pricing. Important for value assessment and profitability analysis.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is actual achieved price per sqft? Realized value? Real market PSF? | Tell me the actual price per square foot achieved from sales.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Calculated from total revenue divided by total saleable area sold. Includes all sold units (booked + completed). Weighted by actual sold quantity.

layer1_attributes.description: Average revenue generated per sold unit. Reflects market value and pricing strategy. Used for ROI calculations and unit profitability analysis.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Average price per unit? Revenue per unit? Ticket size? | Tell me the average revenue earned per unit sold.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Annual Revenue / Annual Units Sold. Represents average across all unit types and buyers. Includes all revenue (principal only).

layer1_attributes.description: Absolute number of units sold monthly calculated from velocity percentage. Converts percentage absorption to actionable unit count. Used for inventory and pipeline planning.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How many units sell monthly based on velocity? Monthly sales in units? Monthly absorption units? | Tell me how many units per month are being sold.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% × Total Supply = Monthly Units. Applied on Total Supply consistently. Based on last 12-month trend.

layer1_attributes.description: Estimated market value of remaining unsold units at current market prices. Represents potential future revenue and asset value. Important for balance sheet valuations.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Value of unsold units? Remaining inventory worth? Stock valuation? | Tell me the estimated value of units still available for sale.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as Unsold × Unit Size × Current PSF. Assumes current PSF applicable to unsold inventory. PSF and area estimates consistent.

layer1_attributes.description: Percentage of total supply cleared (sold) annually. Indicates market demand intensity and project success. Used for absorption modeling and market analysis.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What % of supply clears annually? Yearly sell-through rate? Annual absorption %? | Tell me what percentage of total supply is sold each year.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Annual Sales / Total Supply. Represents sell-through percentage. 12-month rolling period.

layer1_attributes.description: Projected total time to sell entire supply (both sold + remaining) at current absorption pace. Indicates overall project duration from launch to full clearance.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: How long to sell entire project? Total project sellout time? Full clearance timeline? | Tell me how long until all units are sold.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Total Supply / Annual Sales pace. Assumes constant demand. Does not account for market changes or external shocks.

layer1_attributes.description: Ratio of annual sales rate to total supply expressed as percentage. Indicates how efficiently the project is clearing inventory relative to size. Efficiency > 100% is not possible.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Inventory turnover efficiency? Project clearing rate? Absorption efficiency ratio? | Tell me how efficient the project is at selling units.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Calculation: (Annual Sales × 12) / Supply × 100. Should yield realistic percentage. Data consistency verified.

layer1_attributes.description: Average current price per unit at today's market rates. Reflects current buyer investment requirement and affordability. Used for customer segmentation and targeting.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Current average unit price? Today's typical unit cost? Current ticket size? | Tell me the average price of units at current market rates.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unit Size × Current PSF. Assumes current PSF applicable to all remaining units. Size consistency assumed.

layer1_attributes.description: Average unit price at project launch. Historical reference point for price growth analysis. Shows project positioning at market entry.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Launch period average unit price? Initial pricing? Original ticket size? | Tell me what the average unit price was when the project launched.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unit Size × Launch PSF. Reflects introductory pricing strategy. Historical reference only.

layer1_attributes.description: Price difference per square foot between current and launch pricing. Absolute metric of price appreciation. Positive gap indicates market value growth.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: PSF price increase? Price per sqft gap? How much has price increased per sqft? | Tell me the price increase per square foot since launch.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as Current PSF - Launch PSF. Indicates appreciation per unit area. Market-driven changes captured.

layer1_attributes.description: Actual achieved price per square foot when accounting for all expenses and revenue. Reflects true value realization in market.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Real achieved PSF? True market realization? Effective pricing? | Tell me the realistic price per square foot that was achieved.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Derived from (Total Revenue / Total Area of Sold Units). Accounts for all revenue streams and unit mix.

layer1_attributes.description: Price normalized by unit size ratio. Indicates relative affordability per unit area. Used for cross-project comparisons and market benchmarking.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Price-to-size comparison? Normalized pricing metric? Relative affordability? | Tell me the price-to-size metric for comparison.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Ratio = Current PSF / Average Unit Size. Abstract metric for comparative analysis. Higher ratio = premium positioning.

layer1_attributes.description: Time required to completely sell remaining unsold inventory at current monthly absorption pace. Derived from Unsold Units and Monthly Unit Sales. Critical for project timeline and cash flow forecasting.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: How many months to sell remaining inventory?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Time to clear unsold inventory? How long to sell rest of units? Remaining inventory timeline? | Tell me how many months it will take to sell all units left.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unsold Units / Monthly Units Sold. Assumes constant absorption pace. Does not account for market changes.

layer1_attributes.description: Percentage of total project units that have completed or are in possession stage. Indicates project completion and delivery progress against total scope.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What % of units have been delivered so far?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Possession progress? How many units delivered? Completion percentage? | Tell me what percentage of units have been handed over.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on RTO Units / Project Size. Reflects actual possession completion. Updated monthly after possession batches.

layer1_attributes.description: Percentage of total revenue concentrated from top-selling segment (1BHK, 2BHK, etc.). Indicates market concentration and demand pattern. High concentration = segment-dependent risk.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What % of revenue comes from top segment?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Revenue concentration by segment? Top segment contribution? Market dependence? | Tell me what percentage of revenue is from the biggest selling unit type.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Max(Segment Revenue) / Total Revenue. Reflects buyer preference concentration. Identifies key demand drivers.

layer1_attributes.description: Ratio of project absorption to location/market average absorption. Indicates competitive positioning - ratio > 1 means outperforming market, < 1 means underperforming.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: How does project velocity compare to market?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Competitive absorption ratio? vs market average? Market outperformance? | Tell me how this project is selling compared to similar projects.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Project Velocity / Market Average Velocity. Market average calculated from comparable projects in location.
layer1_attributes.formula_derivation: Project Monthly Velocity / Market Avg Velocity

layer1_attributes.description: Annualized rate of price appreciation per square foot. Indicates market strength and investment value growth momentum. Helps forecast future market prices.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What is annual PSF growth rate?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Annual price appreciation rate? Yearly PSF growth? Price momentum? | Tell me how much price per square foot is growing each year.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on (Current PSF - Launch PSF) / (Project Age in Years). Assumes linear growth. Market-driven appreciation.
layer1_attributes.expected_answer: 8.5% per year, 12.3% per year, 15.6% per year

layer1_attributes.description: Average number of days inventory remains in stock before sale. Indicates how quickly units move. Lower days = faster turnover = strong demand.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: How many days to sell an average unit?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Unit sales cycle time? Days to sell per unit? Inventory holding period? | Tell me how many days on average a unit stays unsold before being purchased.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on 365 / Clearance Rate. Assumes constant demand distribution. Average metric across all units.

layer1_attributes.description: Estimated profit per unit sold (Revenue per Unit - Cost per Unit). Indicates project profitability and developer returns per sale.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What is approximate profit per unit?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Margin per unit? Profit per sale? Developer return per unit? | Tell me the estimated profit or margin made on each unit sold.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on (Revenue per Unit - Total Project Cost / Project Size). Simplified profit calculation. Does not include indirect costs.
layer1_attributes.formula_derivation: Revenue per Unit - (Total Project Cost / Project Size)

layer1_attributes.description: Ratio of revenue to total project cost. Indicates project profitability efficiency. Ratio > 1.5 indicates strong margins, < 0.8 indicates challenging economics.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What is project cost efficiency ratio?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Revenue to cost ratio? Project profitability ratio? Cost effectiveness? | Tell me the ratio of revenue earned to development cost.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Total Revenue / Total Project Cost. Higher ratio = better margins. Reflects project economics strength.

layer1_attributes.description: Combined timeline until full project completion (remaining sales time + possession time). Indicates total project duration from today to final handover.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: What is total remaining project timeline?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Time till project is fully done? Remaining timeline? Project duration left? | Tell me how many months until this project is completely finished.
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on Max(Months to Sell Remaining, Time to Possession). Whichever is longer determines project completion.
layer1_attributes.formula_derivation: MAX(Months to Sell Remaining, Time to Possession)

layer1_attributes.description: Monthly percentage of total supply that gets absorbed (sold). Key indicator of market demand intensity and sales momentum. Calculated as (Sold Units / Total Supply) / Project Duration in Months × 100.
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: What is absorption rate? | Calculate absorption rate | What's the monthly absorption? | How fast are units being absorbed? | absorption rate in | absorption for
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Based on total sold units divided by total supply, normalized by project duration in months. Represents average monthly market uptake.

layer1_attributes.description: Duration in months since official project launch to current date. Indicates project maturity in market. Used for cohort analysis, market response benchmarking, and lifecycle stage assessment.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: How old are the projects (in months from launch)?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: Show project age | How many months since launch? | How long have projects been in market? | Tell me how long each project has been selling (age in months) | project age in | project age for | what is project age
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Age calculated from Launch Date to today. Represents time in market (from first sales). Updated monthly.

layer1_attributes.description: Remaining time from today until expected possession date. Indicates buyer timeline and project completion stage. Critical for investment return calculations and buyer decision-making.
layer1_attributes.sample_prompt: How many months until possession for each project?
layer1_attributes.variation_in_prompt: months until possession | How many months until possession | time to possession | When will possession happen? | How long until handover? | Show time to delivery | Tell me how many months from now until buyers get their units
layer1_attributes.assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as (Possession Date - Current Date) / 30. Assumes RERA dates are accurate. Positive = future, Negative = overdue.
